stimulus: The Cleveland Museum of Art in Cleveland, Ohio, has more than 60,000 works of art. High-resolution images of tens of thousands of those works have been put online through the museum's open-access program. One of the images is of Spring Evening, a watercolor by Charles Burchfield. In a paper, a student claims that putting a work from the museum online increases the number of people who experience that work.
stem: Which finding, if true, would most directly support the underlined claim?

answerOptions.content: The Cleveland Museum of Art holds several other watercolors by Burchfield.
Many visitors to Cleveland describe the museum as worth a visit.
Each year, more people view the online image of Spring Evening than visit the Cleveland Museum of Art in person.
Spring Evening has been praised by some art critics for its delicate handling of light.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. If more people view Spring Evening online than visit the entire museum in person, that directly supports the claim that putting a work online increases the number of people who experience it.

Choice A is incorrect; the number of works the museum holds does not address audience size from online access. Choice B is incorrect; opinions about the museum's overall worth do not address online access to specific works. Choice D is incorrect; praise from critics does not measure how many people experience the work.
